content:
Q5. Can I land with an orbiter?
No. Once the probe is moved to the planet's orbit, it becomes an orbiter, and it stays there for the rest of the game. It can not be moved to the Solar system board again and it can not land on the planet.

custom_metadata.title: Q5. Can I land with an orbiter?
custom_metadata.locator: Q5. Can I land with an orbiter?

content:
Q6. Do I get the landing discount for an opponent's orbiter?
Yes, you can land cheaper as long as there is an orbiter at the planet, and it doesn't matter whom this orbiter belongs to.

custom_metadata.title: Q6. Do I get the landing discount for an opponent's orbiter?
custom_metadata.locator: Q6. Do I get the landing discount for an opponent's orbiter?

content:
Q7. Can landing on moons be discounted?
Yes, consider landing on a moon the same as if you would land on the moon's planet. The only difference is that you need a specific tech to be able to land on moons. So if there is an orbiter at a planet, landing on that planet's moon would cost 1 less energy. Similarly, if you have the tech that gives a discount to landing, this applies to landing on moons too.

custom_metadata.title: Q7. Can landing on moons be discounted?
custom_metadata.locator: Q7. Can landing on moons be discounted?

content:
ORBIT A PLANET
Pay 1 credit and 1 energy to turn one of your probes into an orbiter. This is a main action and can only be performed if your probe is on a space with a planet (other than Earth). To orbit a planet, remove your figure from the solar system board and place it in the orbit of that planet on the planetary board. There is no limit to the number of orbiters that a planet can have around it.

custom_metadata.title: ORBIT A PLANET
custom_metadata.locator: Pay 1 credit and 1 energy to turn one of your probes into an orbiter

content:
LAND ON A PLANET OR MOON
Some planets have one or more moons. You cannot land on these unless some effect or tech allows you to do so. Pay 3 energy to turn one of your probes into a lander. This is a main action and can only be performed if your probe is on a space with a planet (other than Earth). If there is an orbiter at that planet already, this costs only 2 energy instead. It does not matter who the orbiter belongs to. To land on a planet, remove your figure from the solar system board and place it on that planet on the planetary board.

custom_metadata.title: LAND ON A PLANET OR MOON
custom_metadata.locator: Pay 3 energy to turn one of your probes into a lander

content: A probe on a planet space may be turned into an orbiter as a main action.

custom_metadata.title: Orbit Action Base
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: a probe on a planet space may be turned into an orbiter as a main action

content: When a probe is moved to orbit on the planetary board, it becomes an orbiter rather than remaining a probe on the solar system board.

custom_metadata.title: Probe Becomes Orbiter
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: when a probe is moved to orbit on the planetary board, it becomes an orbiter rather than remaining a probe on the solar system board

content: Once a figure becomes an orbiter, it stays there for the rest of the game and cannot later land or return to the solar system board.

custom_metadata.title: Orbiter Permanence
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: once a figure becomes an orbiter, it stays there for the rest of the game and cannot later land or return to the solar system board

content: Landing turns a probe into a lander as a main action and normally costs 3 energy.

custom_metadata.title: Landing Action Base
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: landing turns a probe into a lander as a main action and normally costs 3 energy

content: If an orbiter is already at the planet, landing there costs 1 less energy than normal.

custom_metadata.title: Landing Discount From Existing Orbiter
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: if an orbiter is already at the planet, landing there costs 1 less energy than normal

content: The landing discount applies if any orbiter is present at the planet, regardless of which player owns it.

custom_metadata.title: Orbiter Ownership Does Not Matter
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: the landing discount applies if any orbiter is present at the planet, regardless of which player owns it

content: A player cannot land on a moon unless some effect or technology explicitly allows it.

custom_metadata.title: Moon Landing Requires Access
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: a player cannot land on a moon unless some effect or technology explicitly allows it

content: For discount purposes, landing on a moon is treated the same as landing on that moon's planet.

custom_metadata.title: Moon Landing Uses Planet Discount Logic
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: for discount purposes, landing on a moon is treated the same as landing on that moon's planet

content: Other landing-discount effects that apply to the planet also apply to the moon when moon landing is allowed.

custom_metadata.title: Other Landing Discounts Also Apply To Moons
custom_metadata.normalized_statement: other landing-discount effects that apply to the planet also apply to the moon when moon landing is allowed
